input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Typical Lucene applications often add companion modules such as lucene-analyzers-common or lucene-queryparser, but those are not required to compile or meaningfully exercise lucene-core reachability itself.

If tests continue to exercise MMapDirectory or RamUsageEstimator on modern JDKs, local Gradle/Native Image verification may reveal JVM module-access flags are still needed, but that is test-scope compatibility rather than a deterministic dependency or Docker setup requirement.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: lucene-core 6.0.0 is a self-contained Java library artifact with no Maven dependencies, no native components, and no required external service; the observed failure is a source/API compatibility issue in the existing test scaffold because AttributeImpl gained/required reflectWith(AttributeReflector), not missing setup.
